title: Cool Of The Day

sections.label: Verse 1
lines: The serpent came in cool and low,
"Yea, hath God said?" he breathed to me.
He took the only word I'd know
and bent it like a lowered tree.
"You shall not surely die," he smiled,
"your eyes will open, you'll be wise."
(hath God said)

sections.label: Pre-Chorus
lines: And something in me leaned to look,
a hunger older than my name.

sections.label: Chorus
lines: It was good for food, and pleasant to the eyes,
a tree to make a woman wise.
So I took, and I ate, and the silence opened wide,
and I gave it to the man beside.

sections.label: Verse 2
lines: The skin I never knew was bare,
the light came in and showed us plain.
We sewed the fig leaves, hand in hand,
and learned a shame we could not name.
We heard Him walking through the land,
the cool of evening, and our blame.

sections.label: Pre-Chorus
lines: And something in me longed to run,
to be unseen, to be unsame.

sections.label: Chorus
lines: It was good for food, and pleasant to the eyes,
a tree to make a woman wise.
So I took, and I ate, and the silence opened wide,
and I gave it to the man beside.

sections.label: Bridge
lines: "Where art thou?" called the evening air.
(where art thou)
I said, I heard You, and I hid.
I was afraid, I was laid bare,
afraid of everything I did.
"The woman that You gave to me,"
"The serpent, he beguiled me,"
and no one stayed to own the same,
we passed it down the line like flame.

sections.label: Chorus
lines: It was good for food, and pleasant to the eyes,
a tree to make a woman wise.
But I took, and I ate, and the garden closed its door,
and it is not pleasant anymore.

sections.label: Outro
lines: Upon your belly you will go,
and dust is all you'll ever know.
Cool of the day, cool of the day,
the voice still asks me where I lay.

epigraph: And when the woman saw that the tree was good for food
